content.claim: Only 23% of Finland's Tier 3 (special support) students are fully included in general education classrooms; a majority receive special education in special classes, creating a gap between headline inclusion-tier percentages and actual mainstream integration rates.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Contested by comparative literature; Norway's system shows higher full-inclusion rates, but direct causal comparison is not fully established
Data do not distinguish between part-time mainstream attendance, itinerant specialist support, and special-class placement effectiveness
Interpretation as a 'gap' reflects advocacy framing; placement patterns may reflect appropriate intensity matching rather than systematic under-inclusion
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
